# Webhook Trip Lifecycle

> HTTP webhook delivery for order and driver lifecycle events, allowing external systems to react to ride state changes in real time.

**Category:** Integration · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** webhooks · events · notifications · order-lifecycle · driver

## What this does

HTTP webhook delivery for order and driver lifecycle events, allowing external systems to react to ride state changes in real time.

Specifies 4 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **endpoint_url** *(url, required)* — HTTPS URL that will receive webhook POST requests.
- **events** *(multiselect, optional)* — List of event types this endpoint subscribes to. Empty means all events.
- **mode** *(select, optional)* — Whether this endpoint is live or in test mode.
- **version** *(text, optional)* — API version for the webhook payload format.
- **description** *(text, optional)* — Human-readable label for this endpoint.
- **status** *(select, optional)* — Whether this endpoint is active or disabled.
- **api_credential_id** *(text, optional)* — The API credential this endpoint is scoped to.

## What must be true

- **rule_01:** Webhook endpoints are scoped to an organization; each organization manages its own endpoints.
- **rule_02:** An endpoint may subscribe to a specific list of event types or receive all events (empty events array).
- **rule_03:** The platform delivers events as HTTP POST requests to the registered URL with a JSON payload.
- **rule_04:** Delivery includes the full resource payload for the affected entity (order or driver).
- **rule_05:** Failed deliveries are logged in the webhook request log with status code and response body.
- **rule_06:** Event types are fixed and defined at the platform level; custom event types are not supported.
- **rule_07:** Endpoints can be filtered by mode (live vs test) to separate production and test traffic.

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Endpoint Registered** — when operator provides a valid HTTPS URL, then Endpoint is active and will receive matching events from this point forward.
- **Event Delivered** — when a lifecycle event fires (e.g., order.dispatched, order.completed); one or more active endpoints subscribe to this event type, then External system receives the event payload and can react (e.g., update its own database, trigger actions).
- **Delivery Failed** — when the endpoint URL returns a non-2xx status or times out, then Operator can inspect the log and debug the endpoint configuration.
- **All Events Subscribed** — when endpoint is registered with an empty events list, then Endpoint receives every event type fired by the platform.

## Errors it can return

- `WEBHOOK_URL_INVALID` — The provided webhook URL is not a valid HTTPS URL.
- `WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT_NOT_FOUND` — The specified webhook endpoint could not be found.

## Events

**`order.created`** — An order has been created.
  Payload: `order_id`, `customer_id`, `status`, `pickup_location`, `dropoff_location`

**`order.dispatched`** — An order has been dispatched to a driver.
  Payload: `order_id`, `driver_id`, `dispatched_at`

**`order.driver_assigned`** — A driver has been assigned to an order.
  Payload: `order_id`, `driver_id`

**`order.updated`** — An order's status or data has changed.
  Payload: `order_id`, `status`, `updated_at`

**`order.completed`** — An order has been completed.
  Payload: `order_id`, `driver_id`, `customer_id`

**`order.dispatch_failed`** — Order dispatch failed to find a driver.
  Payload: `order_id`

**`driver.assigned`** — A driver has been assigned to an entity or order.
  Payload: `driver_id`, `order_id`

## Connects to

- **ride-request-lifecycle** *(required)* — Lifecycle events are the source of webhook delivery.
- **multi-tenant-organization** *(required)* — Webhook endpoints are scoped per organization.
- **fleet-ops-public-api** *(recommended)* — API credentials scope which endpoints can register webhooks.
